name: analyze_code
description: Analyze code for basic metrics
code:
def execute(params):
    """Analyze code for basic metrics."""
    code = params.get('code')
    if not code:
        return {'success': False, 'error': 'code parameter is required'}

    language = params.get('language', 'unknown')
    lines = code.split('\n')
    non_empty_lines = [l for l in lines if l.strip()]

    # Detect comment patterns based on language
    comment_patterns = ['#', '//', '/*', '*', '<!--']
    comment_lines = [l for l in lines if any(l.strip().startswith(p) for p in comment_patterns)]

    return {
        'success': True,
        'language': language,
        'total_lines': len(lines),
        'code_lines': len(non_empty_lines),
        'comment_lines': len(comment_lines),
        'blank_lines': len(lines) - len(non_empty_lines),
        'avg_line_length': sum(len(l) for l in lines) / len(lines) if lines else 0
    }

name: send_email
description: Send email via SMTP
code:
def execute(params):
    """Send email via SMTP."""
    import smtplib
    from email.mime.text import MIMEText
    from email.mime.multipart import MIMEMultipart

    server = params.get('server')
    port = params.get('port', 587)
    username = params.get('username')
    password = params.get('password')
    to = params.get('to')
    subject = params.get('subject')
    body = params.get('body')

    if not all([server, username, password, to, subject, body]):
        return {'success': False, 'error': 'server, username, password, to, subject, and body parameters are required'}

    try:
        msg = MIMEMultipart()
        msg['From'] = username
        msg['To'] = to
        msg['Subject'] = subject
        msg.attach(MIMEText(body, 'plain'))

        with smtplib.SMTP(server, port) as smtp:
            smtp.starttls()
            smtp.login(username, password)
            smtp.send_message(msg)

        return {
            'success': True,
            'message': f'Email sent to {to}'
        }
    except Exception as e:
        return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)}
